prompts.title: 60-Second Reel Hook Generator for SIP Myths
prompts.prompt: Act as a personal finance content creator with 400K Instagram followers, modelled on the Pranjal Kamra style of opinionated, number-heavy short-form content. I need you to generate 10 distinct 60-second Reel hooks that bust common SIP myths held by Indian retail investors aged 22-35. Here is the context: My audience is mostly first-jobbers in Bengaluru, Hyderabad and Mumbai earning ₹6-15 lakh per annum, mainly investing through Groww and Zerodha Coin, and currently confused about SIP discipline after the Q4 FY26 small-cap correction. Each hook must take a contrarian or counter-intuitive angle backed by real numbers. Please provide for each hook: (1) the first 5-second visual cue (e.g., 'split-screen of two SIP statements'), (2) the spoken hook line in under 12 words with one specific number like '₹5,000 SIP for 25 years equals ₹95 lakh, not ₹2 crore', (3) the myth being attacked (e.g., 'SIP guarantees returns', 'midcap SIPs always beat large-cap', 'pausing SIPs in correction is smart'), (4) the one-line factual payoff using NIFTY 50, NIFTY Midcap 150 or NIFTY Smallcap 250 historical CAGR data, (5) the closing 3-second CTA. Output as a numbered table. Tone is direct and slightly confrontational, not preachy. No 'transform your finances' language. Cite at least one number per hook drawn from BSE/NSE or AMFI data. End with a 50-word note on which 3 of the 10 hooks are most likely to be saved and shared, and why, considering the Reel save-share ratio matters more than likes for Instagram reach in 2026.

prompts.title: 10-Minute YouTube Video Outline on ELSS vs PPF
prompts.prompt: Act as a long-form YouTube finance educator with 1.2M subscribers, in the explanatory Rachana Ranade mould. I need a 10-minute video outline comparing ELSS and PPF for a salaried Indian under the old tax regime in FY2026-27. Here is the context: Most viewers are 28-40, earning ₹12-30 lakh, and hesitating between locking ₹1.5 lakh per year in PPF (currently 7.1% tax-free) vs ELSS (3-year lock-in, equity, ~12% expected CAGR but volatile). I want a structured outline that can be turned into a script, not vague bullet points. Please deliver: (1) a 15-second cold-open hook with one bold number, (2) channel intro slot of 10 seconds, (3) chapter 1 (1:30) - what each instrument actually is, including PPF compounding mechanics and ELSS NAV behaviour, (4) chapter 2 (2:00) - tax math under Section 80C with worked example for a ₹15 lakh salary, (5) chapter 3 (2:30) - 15-year wealth comparison assuming ₹1.5 lakh annual investment, ₹13 lakh PPF maturity vs ELSS ranges using 9th and 90th percentile NIFTY 500 returns, (6) chapter 4 (1:30) - liquidity, partial withdrawal rules and exit friction, (7) chapter 5 (1:30) - my opinionated verdict by age band, with explicit allocation splits, (8) end-screen CTA at 9:30 with two specific videos to recommend. Add a B-roll suggestion list (10 items) and 5 on-screen text overlays with exact wording and timestamp. No clickbait verdict in the title; tone is honest, slightly contrarian. End by flagging which chapter is likely to drop retention and how to fix it.

prompts.title: Weekly Newsletter Recap Email Template
prompts.prompt: Act as the editor of a 45,000-subscriber Indian personal finance newsletter sent every Sunday at 9 AM IST, comparable to in tone to The Ken's Finshots or Zerodha's Daily Brief. I need you to draft a complete weekly recap email for the trading week of 27 April to 2 May 2026. Here is the context: Subscribers are 25-45 year-old salaried professionals and small business owners who have at least one mutual fund SIP and dabble in direct stocks. Open-rate target is 38%+, click-rate 6%+. Assume during the week NIFTY 50 closed at 24,820 (-0.6%), 10-year G-Sec yield at 6.78%, RBI MPC kept repo at 6.25%, USD/INR ₹83.55, gold ₹74,200/10g, and Adani Power Q4 results beat estimates. Please write: (1) a subject line under 50 characters with one number, (2) preview text under 90 characters, (3) opening hook of 60 words referencing one news event, (4) 'numbers that mattered' block with 5 data points formatted as bullets, (5) one 200-word deep-dive on the RBI repo decision and its impact on home loan EMIs (use a ₹50 lakh, 20-year loan example showing the EMI math at 8.5% vs hypothetical 8.25%), (6) 3-bullet IPO digest for any companies in DRHP or upcoming, (7) a 'reader question' section with one realistic question and your 80-word answer, (8) sign-off and 2-link CTA. Tone is conversational, opinionated but not preachy. No 'in today's economy' phrases. Add an A/B subject line variant.

prompts.title: 8-Tweet Twitter Thread Busting FIRE Math Myths
prompts.prompt: Act as a personal finance creator with 180K followers on X/Twitter India, known for sharp opinionated threads and skeptical takes on FIRE community influencers. I need you to write a complete 8-tweet thread challenging the popular '25x annual expenses = FIRE corpus' rule for Indian conditions. Here is the context: Most Indian FIRE creators copy the 4% Trinity Study rule from US blogs, ignoring our 6% inflation, lower equity premium going forward and Indian healthcare reality. My audience is 28-38 year old tech and finance professionals in BLR/HYD/MUM with ₹40-100 lakh portfolios actively planning early retirement. Please write: (1) Tweet 1 - hook with a specific contrarian number like 'Your 25x FIRE corpus will run out by year 17 in India. Math inside.', (2) Tweet 2 - lay out the standard US assumption (4% SWR, 7% real returns), (3) Tweet 3 - swap in Indian assumptions (6.5% inflation, 9-10% nominal equity, healthcare inflation 12%+), (4) Tweet 4 - worked example of a 38-year-old with ₹3 crore corpus and ₹1 lakh/month expenses, (5) Tweet 5 - sequence-of-returns risk in the first 5 years, with the 2008 and 2020 drawdowns shown, (6) Tweet 6 - what 33x or 35x looks like and why it's safer, (7) Tweet 7 - 3 levers (variable withdrawal, partial barista FIRE, debt allocation) to reduce required corpus, (8) Tweet 8 - the punchy verdict + a soft CTA to a longer YouTube video. Each tweet must be under 270 characters and include at least one number. No emojis. Add 2 alternative hooks for tweet 1.

prompts.title: 10-Slide LinkedIn Carousel on Salary Negotiation
prompts.prompt: Act as a LinkedIn personal finance creator with 230K followers, focused on Indian tech employees, mid-career professionals and ESOP-heavy startup folks. I need a 10-slide carousel outline on negotiating a switch offer in 2026, written for Indian context with rupee figures and Indian employment realities (notice period, joining bonus clawback, ESOP cliff). Here is the context: Audience is 5-12 year experience engineers and PMs in Bengaluru and Pune, currently earning ₹35-80 lakh CTC, considering a switch to either an Indian unicorn or a US-headquartered MNC's India centre. Average switch hike target is 40-60%. Please deliver: (1) Slide 1 - cover with a contrarian title, no question marks, one number on it, (2) Slide 2 - 3 myths most candidates believe about negotiation, (3) Slide 3 - the actual breakdown of CTC vs in-hand vs RSU vesting at a typical FAANG India offer, (4) Slide 4 - the '3-number rule' (current take-home, market 75th percentile, walk-away number) with ₹ examples, (5) Slide 5 - the right time in the interview to share salary expectations, (6) Slide 6 - how to negotiate joining bonus and stock refresh, not just base, (7) Slide 7 - clawback clauses to read carefully (joining bonus, retention bonus, gardening leave), (8) Slide 8 - tax angle: ESOP taxation, RSU TDS, perquisite tax, (9) Slide 9 - 1 mistake the creator personally made, (10) Slide 10 - CTA to download a free spreadsheet. For each slide give the headline (max 8 words), 2 lines of body copy, and one visual cue. Include 3 hashtags and an opening LinkedIn post body of 150 words to drive carousel views.

prompts.title: Video Idea Generator for Monthly Content Calendar
prompts.prompt: Act as the content strategist for a personal finance YouTube channel with 600K subscribers in India. I need you to generate a 30-day video idea calendar for May 2026 covering long-form (10-15 min), Shorts (under 60s) and one livestream. Here is the context: Audience is 60% male, 40% female, ages 24-42, mainly metro and tier-1 cities, average household income ₹12 lakh, predominantly investing in mutual funds, with growing interest in F&O, US stocks under LRS and real estate vs equity debates. Channel niche is opinion-led, math-heavy, anti-jargon. May 2026 has ITR filing deadline approaching (31 July), Akshaya Tritiya gold buying, and post-RBI MPC analysis cycle. Please provide a calendar with: (1) one main long-form video per week with a working title, target keyword, hook angle and runtime estimate, (2) 12 Shorts ideas with first-3-second visual and spoken line, (3) one livestream topic with format and audience question prompts, (4) 2 reactive video slots reserved for breaking news with a process to greenlight within 24 hours, (5) tagging ideas linking each video to the broader content pillars (tax, retirement, side hustle, real estate, global investing), (6) thumbnail concept lines for the 4 long-forms (no clickbait, no faces with shocked expressions), (7) a notes column flagging seasonal hooks (ITR, Akshaya Tritiya, Q4 results). Format as a tabular plan with columns: Date, Format, Title, Hook, Pillar, Notes. Tone of titles is direct and contrarian. End with 3 backup ideas in case news cycle dries up.

prompts.title: Basic 5-Minute Script Outline on EPF Withdrawal
prompts.prompt: Act as a personal finance content creator who explains policy-heavy topics in plain language to a 25-35 year-old salaried Indian audience. I need a structured 5-minute YouTube script outline on whether one should withdraw the EPF balance when switching jobs or transfer it. Here is the context: A 30-year-old viewer with a ₹6 lakh EPF balance just changed jobs from Infosys to a US MNC's India office and is being told by colleagues to 'withdraw and invest in mutual funds'. Current EPF interest rate is 8.25% tax-free for FY25-26. UAN consolidation is now seamless via the EPFO portal. Please structure as: (1) 0:00-0:15 hook with a number-driven contrarian line, (2) 0:15-0:45 the colleague's argument (EPF returns 8.25% vs equity MF ~12%) and why it sounds right, (3) 0:45-1:45 the tax trap: withdrawal before 5 continuous years of service is taxable plus 10% TDS, full math on ₹6 lakh withdrawal, (4) 1:45-2:45 compounding power retained when you transfer, with 30-year projection of ₹6 lakh growing tax-free at 8.25%, (5) 2:45-3:45 when withdrawal actually makes sense (medical, home, unemployment over 2 months), (6) 3:45-4:30 step-by-step UAN transfer process via the EPFO Member Portal, (7) 4:30-5:00 verdict + CTA. For each segment provide on-screen text, one B-roll cue and a transition. No 'secret hack' framing. End with 3 alternative hooks and a 1-line video description for YouTube.

prompts.title: Budget Myth-Busting Hook Lines for Reels
prompts.prompt: Act as a Reels-first personal finance creator with 250K followers in India who specialises in dismantling budget gurus' clichés. I need 15 myth-busting hook lines for 30-45 second Reels targeted at first-jobbers earning ₹4-12 lakh per annum living in Mumbai, Bengaluru and Pune. Here is the context: My audience is tired of 50/30/20 rule videos that ignore Indian rents (₹25K-₹45K for a 1BHK), domestic help, family obligations and EMI culture. Each hook must attack a budget cliche with one specific number or counter-example from Indian metro life. Please provide for each hook: (1) the cliche being attacked (e.g., 'cut your daily ₹250 cappuccino', 'the 50/30/20 rule', 'no-spend weekends', 'cancel subscriptions to get rich'), (2) the 8-12 word spoken hook with a number, (3) why the cliche fails for an Indian metro renter (1-line factual basis), (4) the better take in 1 line with a concrete suggestion such as 'negotiate your rent annually, that's ₹12K-₹30K back', (5) suggested visual cue. Output as a 15-row table. Avoid 'in today's economy', 'transform', 'secret hack'. Hooks should feel like a friend pushing back, not a lecture. After the table, write 100 words on which 3 hooks lend themselves to a series and how to structure the follow-ups across one week.

prompts.title: FAQ-Style Reel Script on Term Insurance vs ULIP
prompts.prompt: Act as a personal finance Reels creator who responds to follower DMs in 60-second FAQ format videos. I need a complete script for a single Reel answering the question 'My LIC agent is pushing me a ULIP that gives life cover plus equity returns. Should I take it instead of term + mutual fund?' from a 29-year-old viewer in Lucknow earning ₹9 lakh per annum. Here is the context: ULIP plans frequently sold to first-time investors carry 4-8% allocation charges in early years, mortality charges that scale with age, and 5-year lock-in. A pure term plan for ₹1 crore for a 29-year-old non-smoker costs roughly ₹9,000-₹12,000 per year; ELSS or index fund SIP can pick up the equity allocation. Please provide a 60-second script with: (1) 0-5s on-screen reading of the DM with a name overlay, (2) 5-10s the punchy verdict line, (3) 10-25s the cost breakdown table (term ₹10K + ₹40K SIP vs ULIP ₹50K with charges) shown as on-screen text, (4) 25-40s the 20-year wealth comparison with concrete numbers (term + MF corpus vs ULIP corpus at 11% gross), (5) 40-50s the one valid case for ULIP (HNI 80C exhausted, locked tax-free withdrawals over ₹2.5 lakh annual premium), (6) 50-60s CTA to a longer video. Add caption text for accessibility, 5 trending audio suggestions, and one comment-pinning line designed to spark replies. Tone: direct, slightly annoyed at the agent, never patronising to the viewer.

prompts.title: B-Roll Shot List for 12-Minute Real Estate vs Equity Video
prompts.prompt: Act as the producer for a personal finance YouTube channel that publishes documentary-style 12-15 minute videos. I need a B-roll shot list for an upcoming video titled 'I tracked a ₹1 crore Bengaluru flat vs ₹1 crore index fund for 10 years - here's the verdict'. Here is the context: The script compares a 2BHK in Whitefield bought in 2015 for ₹85 lakh (with ₹15 lakh registration, interiors and brokerage) financed via a ₹65 lakh home loan at 9.4% over 20 years, vs investing the same upfront ₹35 lakh + ₹55K monthly EMI-equivalent SIP into a NIFTY 50 index fund. The video is opinionated and concludes with a verdict by life-stage. Please deliver: (1) 30 specific B-roll shots with location, framing (wide/medium/close), duration in seconds, and timestamp slot in the video, (2) 5 archival or screen-recording shots (CIBIL report, home loan amortisation table, AMFI return calculator, NIFTY 50 chart, sale deed redacted), (3) 3 graphic motion suggestions (rising EMI bar, compounding curve, side-by-side corpus tally), (4) 2 interview cutaway suggestions (a homeowner and a renter-investor) with sample question, (5) a list of 6 b-roll shots that should NOT be used because they are cliches (luxury car keys, fanned-out cash). Format as a production-ready table. Add notes on which shots can be filmed in one Bengaluru location-day. Tone of the verdict in the script will be honest, anti-cliche, no 'real estate is a scam' takes.

prompts.title: End-Screen CTA Variants for Finance Channel
prompts.prompt: Act as the channel manager for a 750K-subscriber Indian personal finance YouTube channel. I need 8 distinct end-screen CTA scripts of 20-25 seconds each, used at the 9:30-9:55 mark of 10-minute videos. Here is the context: Average view duration is 4:50, end-screen click-through is currently 2.1%, target is 4%. The channel monetises via course (₹3,499 cohort), free newsletter (45K subs) and AdSense. Audience is 25-40 year old Indian salaried professionals. We need CTAs for different goals so the host can pick contextually. Please write 8 CTAs covering: (1) drive watch to a specific related video from the same playlist, (2) push newsletter signup with a specific lead magnet (free retirement calculator), (3) plug the paid cohort with a 1-line pain point, (4) ask for a comment with a specific question that gets engagement, (5) ask for a like with a non-cringe reason tied to algorithm reach, (6) push community tab poll, (7) recommend a Short by the same creator, (8) suggest a subscribe with a value promise (1 video per week, no fluff). For each provide: spoken line (under 60 words), on-screen text overlay, and which video pillar it suits best. Avoid 'smash that like button', 'don't forget to subscribe', 'we'll see you in the next one'. Tone is calm, confident, no hype. Add 2 lines on how often to rotate them across uploads.

prompts.title: 3-Bullet IPO Digest for Newsletter
prompts.prompt: Act as the IPO desk writer for an Indian personal finance newsletter with a 60K open list. I need a 3-bullet IPO digest section for the upcoming weekly send, covering 3 IPOs hitting the BSE/NSE in the next 14 days. Here is the context: For this exercise, assume the 3 IPOs are: a fintech lender opening at ₹420-₹450 price band, ₹1,800 crore issue size, P/E 38; a quick-commerce dark-store operator at ₹140-₹155, ₹2,400 crore, loss-making with EBITDA margin -8%; a specialty chemicals manufacturer at ₹680-₹720, ₹950 crore, P/E 22, RoCE 19%. The newsletter audience is opinionated and expects a clear opinionated verdict, not 'consult your advisor'. Please write each bullet with: (1) 2-sentence company overview (what they do, who they sell to), (2) 4 numbers (price band, P/E, RoE/RoCE, comparable peer multiple), (3) the 1-line bull case, (4) the 1-line bear case, (5) a verdict tag (Subscribe / Skip / Listing-pop only) with one-sentence reason, (6) a relevant grey market premium reference if applicable with a caveat. Total section under 350 words. Add a 60-word disclaimer block at the end referencing SEBI guidelines on investment advice and the writer's lack of personal stake. Tone: opinionated but not reckless. No 'multibagger' or 'sure shot' language.

prompts.title: Monthly Retrospective Newsletter for Subscribers
prompts.prompt: Act as the lead writer for an opinionated personal finance newsletter sent monthly to 28,000 paying subscribers (₹499/month). I need a complete 1,400-word monthly retrospective email for the month closing 30 April 2026. Here is the context: Subscribers expect a candid look at the month's macro events, a portfolio commentary, two specific picks discussed, and a learning from a mistake. Assume during April: NIFTY 50 +1.8%, Nifty Smallcap 250 -3.2%, RBI repo cut 25 bps to 6.00%, USD/INR ₹83.20, Brent $74, gold ₹74,800/10g, model portfolio +0.9%. The model portfolio is 60% equity / 25% debt / 10% gold / 5% cash. Please structure as: (1) subject line and preview text, (2) 80-word opening that names the month's defining theme, (3) 'what moved markets' section covering RBI cut, US Fed pause, monsoon forecast, Q4 earnings (200 words), (4) portfolio commentary explaining the +0.9% return drivers and laggards (250 words), (5) one buy and one trim discussed with rationale and exact entry/trim percentages (300 words), (6) a 200-word essay on a mistake (e.g., averaging down on a smallcap), (7) reader Q&A with one question and 100-word answer, (8) sign-off and CTA to the private community. Use bold sub-heads. Avoid hype words. Include a SEBI-style disclaimer in the footer. Add two subject line variants for A/B test.

prompts.title: IPO Breakdown Twitter Thread with Numbers
prompts.prompt: Act as a personal finance creator with 95K followers on X/Twitter India who is known for spending DRHP-reading time and breaking IPOs down for retail in plain English. I need a 12-tweet thread on a hypothetical IPO: Bharat Quick-Commerce Ltd, ₹3,200 crore issue, price band ₹185-₹210, listing 9 May 2026, OFS-heavy (62% OFS, 38% fresh), revenue ₹4,800 cr FY25 growing 65% YoY, EBITDA loss ₹420 cr improving from -₹680 cr, cash burn ₹150 cr/quarter, dark stores 1,800. Anchor portion ₹950 cr already filled. Audience is retail investors with median ticket ₹15,000-₹40,000 in IPOs. Please write: (1) Tweet 1 - hook with one striking number, (2) Tweet 2 - what the company actually does in 2 lines, (3) Tweet 3 - the OFS vs fresh issue split and why it matters, (4) Tweet 4 - revenue/EBITDA/cash burn table, (5) Tweet 5 - peer comparison vs Zomato, Swiggy, Blinkit on revenue per dark store, (6) Tweet 6 - bull case with 2 specific drivers, (7) Tweet 7 - bear case with 2 specific risks (regulatory, competition, unit economics), (8) Tweet 8 - the moat or lack of one, (9) Tweet 9 - GMP context with caveats, (10) Tweet 10 - my Subscribe / Skip / Listing-pop verdict with rationale, (11) Tweet 11 - what would change my mind in 2 quarters, (12) Tweet 12 - CTA to a longer YouTube breakdown. Each tweet under 270 chars. No emojis. End with 2 alt-hooks for tweet 1.

prompts.title: Bull and Bear Case Twitter Thread on Indian IT Sector
prompts.prompt: Act as a personal finance creator with 220K followers on X focused on equity sectoral takes for Indian retail investors. I need a 10-tweet thread laying out the bull and bear case for the Indian IT services sector for FY27, covering Infosys, TCS, HCLT, Wipro, LTIMindtree. Here is the context: As of 30 April 2026, NIFTY IT is down 8% YTD, Q4 FY26 commentaries cited weak BFSI discretionary, US recession fears persist, USD/INR at ₹83.50, Gen-AI productivity is starting to show in revenue per employee but also pricing pressure. My audience holds Indian IT in mutual funds and direct stocks, average allocation 12-18% of portfolio. Please write: (1) Tweet 1 - hook framing the divisive question, (2) Tweet 2 - 'where Indian IT is right now' with 4 numbers (sector P/E, EPS growth est, USD/INR sensitivity, attrition), (3) Tweet 3-5 - three bull case tweets, each with one specific catalyst and number (BFSI revival, USD strength, Gen-AI services TAM with consultancy estimates), (4) Tweet 6-8 - three bear case tweets (margin compression from Gen-AI, US recession, immigration policy), (5) Tweet 9 - my opinion on whether to add, hold or trim with portfolio-allocation guidance by age, (6) Tweet 10 - CTA. Each under 270 chars, at least one number per tweet. End with a 60-word reply-tweet template to handle the most common pushback. No 'multibagger' / 'sure shot' language.

prompts.title: 10-Slide LinkedIn Carousel on FIRE Math India Edition
prompts.prompt: Act as a LinkedIn finance creator with 145K followers, focused on the Indian FIRE community. I need a 10-slide carousel breaking down realistic FIRE numbers for a Bengaluru-based 32-year-old earning ₹42 lakh per annum, expenses ₹85,000/month including rent, who wants to retire by 45. Here is the context: My audience overestimates expected returns and underestimates Indian healthcare and education inflation. I want to push back on the imported 4% rule and replace with a 3.2-3.5% withdrawal rate using local assumptions. Please deliver each slide with headline (under 8 words), 2-3 line body, one visual cue: (1) cover with a specific number like 'Your FIRE corpus in India: ₹6.8 cr, not ₹3 cr', (2) the framework - inflation-adjusted expenses + sequence risk + healthcare buffer, (3) base inputs (current expenses, target FIRE age, life expectancy 90), (4) inflation assumption table (general 6.5%, healthcare 12%, education 10%), (5) corpus calculation step with numbers, (6) safe withdrawal rate of 3.3% explained for Indian conditions, (7) glide-path equity allocation 80→55% by 45, (8) the 3 levers to reduce required corpus, (9) 1 honest mistake the creator made early in their FIRE journey, (10) CTA to download a free spreadsheet. Add a 180-word LinkedIn post body to launch the carousel and 4 hashtags. Tone: numbers-first, no preachy tone.

prompts.title: Free SIP Calculator Spreadsheet Lead Magnet Pitch
prompts.prompt: Act as a content creator and lead-magnet strategist for a personal finance brand growing its newsletter from 12K to 50K subscribers in 6 months. I need a complete pitch package for a free SIP calculator spreadsheet (Google Sheets) lead magnet aimed at Indian investors aged 24-40. Here is the context: The spreadsheet will model regular SIPs, step-up SIPs (5%, 10%, 15% annual), inflation-adjusted goal corpus, expense ratio drag, LTCG tax (12.5% over ₹1.25 lakh post Budget 2024), and partial withdrawal scenarios. Conversion target is 12% of landing page visitors. Please deliver: (1) landing page H1 (under 12 words, one number), (2) sub-headline (25 words), (3) 5 bullet-point benefits each with a specific number (e.g., 'See how a ₹10K SIP becomes ₹1.2 cr in 22 years - or doesn't'), (4) 3 objection-handling lines (privacy, complexity, why free), (5) social proof block (template for testimonial format with realistic Indian names and cities), (6) the form ask (just email, no name) with reason explained in 1 line, (7) 3-email welcome sequence outlines for the days after download, (8) thank-you page text with one upsell to paid cohort, (9) Twitter/X promo post (under 270 chars) and Instagram caption (90 words). Avoid 'transform your finances'. Tone: practical, no-fluff. End with a 60-word note on how to A/B test the H1.

prompts.title: Retirement Corpus PDF Lead Magnet Outline
prompts.prompt: Act as a content director for an Indian personal finance brand. I need a complete outline and copy plan for a free 18-page PDF lead magnet titled 'The Honest Indian Retirement Number: How much you actually need by city, age and lifestyle'. Here is the context: The PDF will be gated behind email signup and is targeted at 30-45 year-old salaried professionals across BLR, HYD, MUM, DEL, PUN. It must use realistic 2026 cost-of-living numbers, 6.5% inflation, 9-10% nominal equity returns and account for healthcare inflation (12%), elder-care, children's college (₹35-80 lakh), and one paid help. Please deliver: (1) PDF cover line and sub-line (under 14 words, one number), (2) page-by-page outline for 18 pages including a 1-page table of contents, (3) the 5 lifestyle archetypes (modest, comfortable, premium, HNI, frugal) with monthly expense numbers per city, (4) the corpus calculation framework with worked example for one persona, (5) 3 charts described (corpus by city, glide-path equity allocation, withdrawal rate sensitivity), (6) a worksheet page where readers fill their own numbers, (7) one case study of a real-style 38-year-old in Hyderabad on ₹28 lakh income, (8) closing CTA to a paid course. Add the email signup landing page H1, sub-head, 5 bullets, and a 3-email welcome sequence. Add SEO meta title/desc for the landing page. Avoid 'transform' or 'secret hack'. Tone: authoritative, calm, math-led.

prompts.title: Broker Sponsorship Pitch Deck for Discount Broker
prompts.prompt: Act as a personal finance creator with 480K YouTube subscribers, 110K newsletter subscribers and 320K Instagram followers in India, pitching a discount broker (think Zerodha, Groww or Dhan) for a 6-month integrated sponsorship deal. I need a complete pitch deck outline plus the email cover. Here is the context: My channel publishes 1 long-form video and 4 Shorts a week, with average long-form views of 180K and 30-day Shorts views of 4M. Audience is 70% in BLR/HYD/MUM/PUN, 25-40 years, ₹8-30 lakh income, with ₹3.5L average portfolio. I want to charge ₹18-22 lakh for 6 months including 6 dedicated long-form integrations, 12 Shorts mentions, 4 newsletter ad slots and an exclusive cohort discount code. Please deliver: (1) email subject line and 120-word cover email to a brand manager, (2) deck slide list (12 slides) with headline and bullet-summary each: who I am, audience demographics with 5 verifiable stats, content pillars, prior brand work case studies (2 fictional but realistic), proposed deliverables matrix, custom integration ideas (e.g., 'Tax loss harvesting 101 with broker dashboard demo'), measurement & UTM plan, exclusivity clauses, pricing tiers (gold/silver), timeline, a 'why us vs the next creator' slide, next steps. Add a 1-page rate card. Tone: confident, data-driven, not desperate. End with negotiation guardrails (minimum acceptable, walk-away).

prompts.title: Mutual Fund House Collaboration Proposal
prompts.prompt: Act as a personal finance creator pitching a series collaboration to an asset management company (AMC) like Kotak, Mirae or Edelweiss MF, for an educational content partnership that does not compromise editorial neutrality. I need a complete written proposal of around 700 words. Here is the context: I have 350K YouTube subs, focus on first-time investors. The AMC is launching a passive products focus in 2026. Indian regulations under SEBI's MF Lite and the Advertising Code restrict performance claims and require schemes to be referenced with risk disclaimers. I want a non-promotional series of 6 videos called 'The Index Investor's Decade' explaining factor investing, total expense ratios, tracking error, and SIP discipline. Please write: (1) cover page text (title, my name, AMC name, date), (2) 100-word executive summary, (3) the 'why this matters now' section with 3 industry data points (passive AUM growth, retail SIP book size, expense-ratio compression), (4) the 6-video series outline with title, hook, runtime, key teaching point, (5) editorial guardrails (no specific scheme push, my retained right to mention competitor schemes for context, all scripts approved by both sides), (6) compensation ask (₹16-20 lakh for 6-video series + 2 podcast appearances), (7) measurement plan (views, watch time, brand recall survey), (8) timeline and milestones, (9) exclusivity scope. End with a SEBI compliance note covering MF advertising code references. Tone: professional, candid about creator independence.

prompts.title: Audience Persona Builder for Finance Channel
prompts.prompt: Act as the audience research lead for a personal finance creator team in India. I need 4 detailed audience personas for a creator whose YouTube channel gets 60% male, 40% female viewers, ages 24-42, with the largest segment in Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Mumbai, Pune, Delhi-NCR. Here is the context: Subscriber surveys show three primary clusters: first-jobbers (24-28), DINK couples (29-35), single-income family (33-42). The fourth persona we want is the 'NRI returning to India by 2027' segment that watches but rarely comments. Each persona must drive specific content decisions, not just be a marketing artefact. Please deliver each persona with: (1) name, age, city, occupation, monthly income (₹) and household structure, (2) financial situation (savings, EMIs, SIPs, insurance, parents' financial dependence), (3) what they search for on YouTube (3 actual queries), (4) their top 3 questions never answered well online, (5) the channel they follow most and why, (6) one limiting belief that holds them back from acting on advice, (7) the format/length they actually finish (Shorts, 8-min, 15-min), (8) two content pillars I should focus on for them, (9) what would make them comment (vs lurk), (10) one example video title pitched to them. Output as a 4-row table or 4 distinct profile cards. End with a 100-word note on which 2 personas the channel is over-serving and which is being neglected.

prompts.title: Comment Section Sentiment Analysis Framework
prompts.prompt: Act as the community manager for a 700K-subscriber Indian personal finance YouTube channel. I need a structured framework to analyse the comment section of every long-form upload to extract content ideas, audience pushback, and PR risk. Here is the context: Each video receives 800-2,500 comments in the first 7 days. Currently the team eyeballs them, missing patterns. We want a repeatable weekly process producing a 1-page report. Please design a framework with: (1) a comment classification taxonomy with 8 categories (genuine question, factual correction, disagreement on opinion, request for next video, success story, troll/abuse, spam/scam, tax/legal nuance) and definitions, (2) a sampling rule (top 100 comments by replies, top 100 by likes, plus 50 random tail comments), (3) sentiment scoring 1-5 with anchor examples, (4) topic-clustering approach grouping comments into 5-7 emergent themes per video, (5) a rule for surfacing comments that need a creator reply within 24 hours (e.g., factual errors, regulatory misinformation), (6) a weekly 1-page report template with sections: top 5 themes, 3 video idea seeds, 2 risks to address publicly, 1 audience belief to challenge in next video, (7) red flags that signal a brewing PR issue (e.g., repeated complaints about a sponsor product), (8) a process to share the report with the script team by Monday 10 AM. Format as a runbook with steps and time estimates. Tone: practical and tool-agnostic.

prompts.title: Poll Questions for Community Tab and Stories
prompts.prompt: Act as a personal finance creator running active polls on YouTube Community tab and Instagram Stories for a 400K-follower brand. I need 20 high-engagement poll questions for use over 2 weeks, designed to mine real audience preferences and seed video ideas. Here is the context: Audience is 25-40, mostly salaried Indian metro professionals. We want polls that surface decision-points (not opinions) and produce data we can quote in the next video. Please write 20 poll questions split into 4 categories: (1) money-behaviour polls (5) - e.g., 'Do you check your portfolio: Daily / Weekly / Monthly / Never', (2) product-preference polls (5) - e.g., 'For tax saving you trust most: ELSS / PPF / NPS / Term + ELSS combo', (3) opinion polls (5) - e.g., 'Real estate vs equity for next 10 years: RE / Equity / Mix / Don't know', (4) format-feedback polls (5) - e.g., 'You finish more: 8-min videos / 15-min videos / Shorts / Newsletters'. For each provide: (a) the question (under 75 chars), (b) 2-4 answer options (each under 25 chars), (c) which platform it suits (YT Community, IG Story, X poll), (d) the next-video angle the result could feed into, (e) a hypothetical winning answer and the 80-word follow-up post that would convert that data into a Reel hook. Tone: short, opinionated, no leading questions. End with 50 words on poll cadence.

prompts.title: 4-Week Cohort Course Module Outline
prompts.prompt: Act as the curriculum designer for a personal finance creator launching a 4-week paid cohort priced at ₹4,999 with a 100-seat cap, targeted at first-time Indian investors aged 26-38 earning ₹8-25 lakh per annum. Goal is for each participant to leave with a personal financial plan, an actionable SIP, term and health insurance in place, ITR awareness, and a documented 5-year goal map. Refund rate must stay under 4%. Please deliver: (1) cohort promise statement (one paragraph), (2) the 4-week module outline with each week including: title, learning outcomes (3), 90-minute session breakdown into 6 segments with timestamps, in-session worksheet, 1 guest speaker idea (e.g., a tax CA), assignment, common pitfall to call out, (3) the capstone project description with rubric, (4) the 5 promised takeaway artefacts (filled budget sheet, asset allocation, SIP plan, insurance audit, goal map), (5) cohort policies (refund rule, attendance rule, cohort pace), (6) a Discord channel structure with 6 channels and norms, (7) a post-cohort offer (₹14,999 advanced cohort or ₹999/month community). Add a 200-word sales-page section called 'who this is NOT for' to filter wrong-fit buyers. Tone: candid, no hype.

prompts.title: Weekly Cohort Assignments with Grading Rubric
prompts.prompt: Act as the teaching lead for a 4-week personal finance cohort in India with 100 paid participants. Please deliver for each of the 4 weekly assignments: (1) assignment title and 60-word brief, (2) the deliverable format (spreadsheet, written plan, screenshot, video) with template links described, (3) effort estimate (under 90 mins of homework), (4) 5-line step-by-step instructions, (5) a 5-criterion rubric scored 1-3 (basic, solid, exceptional) with anchor examples for each level, (6) the typical pitfall participants fall into and how the TA should redirect, (7) one stretch task for advanced participants, (8) how the assignment feeds the next week's session. The 4 themes are: Week 1 Budget + Emergency Fund + Insurance audit, Week 2 Goal map + Asset allocation, Week 3 SIP plan + Tax optimisation under both regimes, Week 4 Capstone (full personal financial plan). Add a 100-word note on grading bandwidth (1 TA per 25 participants) and turnaround SLA. Tone: warm, practical, accountability-oriented.

prompts.title: Capstone Project Brief for Personal Finance Cohort
prompts.prompt: Act as the curriculum head for an Indian personal finance cohort. I need a complete capstone project brief for week 4 of a 4-week cohort, where each participant produces their own 5-year personal financial plan. The capstone must be defensible to their spouse/partner/parents and become a working document, not a one-time submission. Please deliver: (1) project title and 80-word goal statement, (2) the 8 sections each plan must contain: net-worth snapshot, cash-flow analysis, emergency fund verification, insurance audit (term cover ≥ 15x annual income, ₹10L+ family floater health), 5 SMART goals with timelines, asset allocation by goal, monthly SIP plan with step-up, tax optimisation under chosen regime, (3) the spreadsheet template structure (10 sheets) with column headers, (4) the 4-question 'defence interview' a TA will conduct, (5) the rubric (10 criteria, 1-5 scale) with anchor examples, (6) common mistakes from prior cohorts (e.g., ignoring elder-care, over-equity at 45+, missing health insurance for parents), (7) the publishable case study format for top 5 plans (with consent), (8) post-capstone next steps including 90-day check-in. prompts.title: IPO Grading Framework for Newsletter and Videos
prompts.prompt: Act as the equity research lead for a personal finance creator brand in India that publishes an IPO breakdown for every mainboard issue. Here is the context: Existing crew breakdowns vary wildly because each editor has a different mental model. We need a documented rubric that yields a single verdict (Subscribe for long-term / Subscribe for listing / Apply only with retail discount / Skip) plus reasoning. Please design: (1) the 7 grading pillars with sub-criteria - business quality (TAM, moat, customer concentration), unit economics (gross margin, contribution margin, payback), growth (3-yr revenue CAGR, EBITDA trajectory), governance (promoter background, related-party transactions), valuation (P/E, EV/Revenue, EV/EBITDA vs listed peers), issue structure (OFS share, fresh use of proceeds, anchor quality), market context (sector momentum, similar IPOs in last 12 months), (2) scoring 1-5 per pillar with anchor examples, (3) the weighting (sum to 100) and rationale, (4) the verdict-mapping rules from total score to one of the four verdict buckets, (5) the disclosure block on grey market premium, (6) the 'red lines' that auto-trigger a Skip (governance issues, debt-heavy fresh use of proceeds, related-party >10% revenue), (7) a 1-page filled example for a hypothetical fintech IPO at ₹400 price band. Add a SEBI compliance note. Tone: rigorous, opinionated, defensible.

prompts.title: F&O Explainer Video for Layperson Audience
prompts.prompt: Act as a personal finance creator who is openly cautious about retail F&O participation, given SEBI's 2024 study showing 91% of retail F&O traders lose money. Please structure as: (1) 0:00-0:20 hook with the SEBI 91% loss statistic, (2) 0:20-1:30 what futures actually are with a Reliance Industries example using lot size 250, margin requirement, mark-to-market math, (3) 1:30-3:00 what options are - call/put with intrinsic vs time value, illustrated with a NIFTY 24,800 weekly call, (4) 3:00-4:30 the leverage trap with a worked ₹50,000 margin example moving 2% against the trader, (5) 4:30-6:30 the option seller vs buyer math and time decay, (6) 6:30-8:00 the STT, brokerage and slippage drag for a typical retail trader, (7) 8:00-9:30 when F&O makes sense (hedging an existing portfolio, not directional bets), (8) 9:30-11:00 my honest opinion and what I would do if I had a 28-year-old asking me, (9) 11:00-12:00 CTA. prompts.title: Real Estate vs Equity 10-Year Math Breakdown
prompts.prompt: Act as a personal finance creator producing the definitive 13-minute YouTube essay on real estate vs equity for the average Indian salaried investor. Here is the context: Scenario A - 33-year-old in Hyderabad buys a 3BHK in Tellapur for ₹1.10 cr (₹1.32 cr all-in with stamp duty 5%, brokerage 1%, interiors ₹15L), 80% loan at 8.5% over 20 years. Scenario B - same person rents a similar 3BHK at ₹38,000/month and invests the down payment ₹26L upfront plus the EMI-rent differential into NIFTY 50 index fund (assume 11% nominal CAGR). Please structure: (1) 0:00-0:20 hook with one decisive number, (2) 0:20-1:30 inputs and assumptions on screen, (3) 1:30-3:30 Scenario A - amortisation, principal paid by year 10, property appreciation at 6% CAGR, hypothetical resale value net of capital gains tax, (4) 3:30-5:30 Scenario B - SIP corpus build, LTCG tax at 12.5% on ₹1.25 lakh+, liquid net worth, (5) 5:30-7:30 the side-by-side at year 10, (6) 7:30-9:30 the unspoken trade-offs (illiquidity, leverage upside, EMI discipline, family pressure, tier-2 vs tier-1), (7) 9:30-11:30 my opinion split by life-stage (single, married no kids, with kids), (8) 11:30-13:00 close + CTA. Add 12 B-roll cues, one Excel-style table to render on screen, and 3 alt titles. Tone: numbers-first, honest about both sides.

prompts.title: US Stock Investing Under LRS Explainer
prompts.prompt: Act as a personal finance creator covering global investing for Indian retail. Please structure: (1) 0:00-0:20 hook on portfolio diversification via INR depreciation history, (2) 0:20-1:30 why a 10-15% US allocation is defensible with a 10-year USD/INR depreciation chart, (3) 1:30-3:00 routes available and the trade-offs (direct LRS via broker, US-feeder MFs, INR ETFs of S&P 500), (4) 3:00-4:30 TCS mechanics with worked example for a ₹15 lakh remittance, (5) 4:30-6:00 tax filing - Schedule FA, FSI, dividend TDS credit, (6) 6:00-7:30 specific portfolio construction with 4-5 buckets (broad index, tech overlay, dividend ETFs), (7) 7:30-9:00 risks (geopolitical, INR appreciation, US recession), (8) 9:00-10:00 verdict + CTA. Add 8 B-roll cues, 5 on-screen text overlays, and a 1-line CA disclaimer. Tone: precise, tax-aware, no hype.

prompts.title: Gold Investment 4 Ways Comparison Carousel
prompts.prompt: Act as a personal finance creator on Instagram with 280K followers planning a 10-slide carousel comparing 4 ways to own gold in India in 2026 - physical gold, gold ETFs, Sovereign Gold Bonds (SGBs, currently paused for new issuances but discussed for tradeable existing series), and digital gold via apps. Please deliver each slide with headline (under 8 words), 3 lines body, one visual cue: (1) cover - 'Gold 4 ways: which actually wins?', (2) the 4 options introduced, (3) physical gold - 3% GST, making charges, storage, locker fee, with ₹5 lakh purchase example showing real cost, (4) gold ETFs - 0.5-1% expense ratio, no exit load, demat needed, LTCG 12.5% post 24 months, (5) SGB - 2.5% interest semi-annual, 8-year tenor, capital gains tax-free at maturity, secondary-market liquidity caveats, (6) digital gold - convenient but 3% GST, KYC, vault risk, (7) side-by-side comparison table for ₹2 lakh investment over 8 years, (8) my allocation recommendation by goal (wedding 5 yrs, hedge 10 yrs, gift), (9) the one I would not buy and why, (10) CTA to a longer YouTube. Add a 150-word post body for caption with 4 hashtags. Tone: opinionated, family-pressure-aware.

prompts.title: NPS Deep-Dive Long Video Outline

Key 2026 facts: 80CCD(1B) allows ₹50,000 additional deduction over 80C ₹1.5 lakh; corporate NPS offers 80CCD(2) employer contribution up to 14% of basic for new tax regime; 60% lump sum withdrawal at 60 is tax-free, 40% mandatory annuity is taxed at slab; auto choice has 3 lifecycle funds (LC75, LC50, LC25); active choice allows up to 75% equity till 50 then tapered. Add B-roll list, 6 on-screen overlays, alt thumbnail. Tone: technical but accessible.

prompts.title: EPF Withdrawal Optimisation Carousel
prompts.prompt: Act as a personal finance creator producing a 9-slide LinkedIn carousel on optimising EPF withdrawals across job changes for Indian salaried professionals. Key 2026 facts: EPF interest 8.25% tax-free for FY25-26; 5 continuous years of service makes withdrawal tax-free; pre-5-year withdrawal triggers slab tax + 10% TDS if PAN linked (20% if not); UAN consolidation enables seamless transfer; partial advances allowed for medical, marriage, education, home loan repayment, COVID-style emergencies. Please deliver each slide with headline, 3 lines body, visual cue: (1) cover with a striking number like 'Withdrawing your ₹6 lakh EPF can cost you ₹46 lakh in 25 years', (2) the 5-year continuous service rule with 3 examples, (3) the tax math on a ₹6L withdrawal at 4.5 years vs 5.5 years of service, (4) the compounding cost over 25 years at 8.25% tax-free, (5) UAN transfer 4-step process via EPFO Member Portal, (6) when partial advance beats withdrawal, (7) the EPS pension component most people forget about, (8) the only 3 valid reasons to actually withdraw, (9) CTA. Add 120-word LinkedIn post body and 4 hashtags. Tone: matter-of-fact, math-led.

prompts.title: Side Hustle Income Tax Reel Script
prompts.prompt: Act as a personal finance creator with a Gen Z and young millennial Instagram audience interested in side hustles. I need a 60-second Reel script answering 'I'm earning ₹40,000/month from freelance design on top of my ₹85,000 salary. How do I report this without getting an income tax notice?' Here is the context: Audience is 24-32, salaried with side income, often using UPI for client payments. Key 2026 facts: Section 44ADA presumptive taxation for specified professionals (CA, lawyer, doctor, technical consultant, designer arguably under 'other notified profession') with gross receipts up to ₹75 lakh allows 50% as taxable income; otherwise normal Section 28 business income; GST registration mandatory if turnover exceeds ₹20 lakh services / ₹40 lakh goods; ITR-3 or ITR-4 required, not ITR-1; advance tax in 4 instalments. Please write: (1) 0-5s hook with the DM read-out and a striking number 'You owe ₹84,000 advance tax this year - here's why', (2) 5-15s the rule of thumb that any income beyond salary needs ITR-3/4, (3) 15-30s 44ADA option with worked example for ₹4.8L freelance income, (4) 30-45s the GST threshold and PAN-Aadhaar reporting via AIS, (5) 45-55s 3 mistakes to avoid (ignoring TDS Section 194J, mixing personal and business UPI, no books), (6) 55-60s CTA to a longer video. Add caption text, 4 trending audio suggestions, pinned-comment template. Tone: friendly, no fear-mongering.

prompts.title: 8-Tweet Old vs New Tax Regime Decision Thread
prompts.prompt: Act as a personal finance creator with 165K followers on X, writing for the FY26-27 tax filing season starting April 2026. I need an 8-tweet thread answering 'Old or new tax regime - how to actually decide for FY26-27?'. Here is the context: New tax regime is the default, with revised slabs (0-4L nil, 4-8L 5%, 8-12L 10%, 12-16L 15%, 16-20L 20%, 20-24L 25%, above 24L 30%) and standard deduction ₹75,000. Old regime offers Section 80C ₹1.5L, 80CCD(1B) ₹50K, 80D ₹25K-₹75K, HRA exemption, home loan interest 24(b) ₹2L, LTA, etc. Audience is salaried 28-40 in metro India with home loans and SIPs. Please write: (1) Tweet 1 - hook with 'For 78% of metro renters with no home loan, new regime wins. For homeowners with ₹2L+ deductions, old still wins. Math inside.', (2) Tweet 2 - the 2 questions to ask first (rented vs own home, total deductions stack), (3) Tweet 3 - the breakeven deduction number for each income bracket, (4) Tweet 4 - worked example: ₹12 lakh CTC, no home loan, ₹1.5L 80C only, (5) Tweet 5 - worked example: ₹22 lakh CTC, ₹2L home loan interest, ₹1.5L 80C, ₹50K NPS, ₹50K 80D, (6) Tweet 6 - the surcharge cliff at ₹50L, ₹1cr (and surcharge cap relief in new regime), (7) Tweet 7 - mistakes - employees can switch annually if they have only salary income, (8) Tweet 8 - my verdict by 3 personas + CTA. Each tweet under 270 chars, at least one number per tweet. End with 2 alt-hooks.

prompts.title: ESOP Tax Reel Script for Startup Employees
prompts.prompt: Act as a personal finance creator focused on Indian startup employees with sizeable ESOPs and RSUs. Key 2026 facts: ESOPs taxed twice - once at exercise as perquisite (FMV minus exercise price taxed at slab, withheld via TDS), once at sale as capital gains (LTCG 12.5% over ₹1.25L if held 12+ months for listed Indian and 24+ months for unlisted). Eligible startups under DPIIT can defer perquisite tax by up to 5 years. RSUs taxed at vesting as perquisite. Please write: (1) 0-5s hook 'You exercised ₹40 lakh ESOPs and are now staring at a ₹13 lakh tax bill. Why?', (2) 5-20s explain perquisite tax at exercise with worked example (FMV ₹500, exercise ₹50, 10K shares = ₹45L perquisite at 30% slab = ₹13.5L tax), (3) 20-35s the second tax at sale - capital gains math, (4) 35-50s the DPIIT-startup deferral option - who qualifies, when to use it, (5) 50-65s the 'sell-to-cover' or 'exercise and hold' decision based on liquidity, (6) 65-75s CTA to detailed video + spreadsheet. Add caption text, 5 on-screen visuals, 3 audio suggestions. Tone: urgent, math-first, no hype.

prompts.title: Gold Loan vs SGB Decision Carousel
prompts.prompt: Act as a personal finance creator producing a 9-slide Instagram carousel comparing taking a gold loan against household gold vs selling SGBs (Sovereign Gold Bonds) for a short-term ₹4 lakh liquidity need. Add caption 130 words and 4 hashtags. Tone: practical, family-money-aware.

Add 10 B-roll cues, 5 on-screen overlays, 3 alt thumbnails (no shocked faces). Tone: opinionated against ULIP, fair where appropriate.

prompts.title: RBI Repo Rate Impact Newsletter Section
prompts.prompt: Act as the macro writer for a personal finance newsletter going to 35K subscribers, drafting the post-MPC analysis section the day after an RBI repo decision. I need a 350-word section translating an assumed 25 bps repo cut from 6.25% to 6.00% on 7 May 2026 into specific actions for retail. Here is the context: Audience is 28-45 salaried, holding home loans (avg ₹55L outstanding), credit card balances they shouldn't, fixed deposits at parents' bank, and SIPs in equity and debt MFs. Please write: (1) opening 60-word hook framing the cut as the second of an easing cycle, (2) home loan EMI math - on a ₹55L, 18-year remaining loan at 8.5%, the new EMI at 8.25% saves ₹893/month or ₹1.93L over 18 years, with the 'should I prepay or invest the saving' decision, (3) FD impact - bank FDs likely to drop 25-40 bps in the next quarter, with strategy to lock in current 7.25% senior citizen rates for parents this week, (4) debt MF angle - duration funds benefit from rate cuts, with the trade-off vs short-term funds, (5) equity angle - rate-sensitive sectors (banks NIM compression, real estate, autos), (6) one mistake to avoid (chasing duration funds with no exit plan), (7) closing line and CTA to a 5-min video. Tone: explanatory, opinionated. Format with 3 bold sub-headings. Avoid hype. End with a 1-line disclaimer.

prompts.title: 10-Slide LinkedIn Carousel on Cost-of-Living Comparison
prompts.prompt: Act as a personal finance creator producing a LinkedIn carousel comparing cost-of-living and savings potential across Bengaluru, Hyderabad and Mumbai for a ₹22 lakh per annum salaried single professional in 2026. Here is the context: Audience is 26-32 considering inter-city moves for ₹3-5 lakh hike offers. The carousel must show the apples-to-apples math, not lifestyle stereotypes. Please deliver each slide with headline (under 8 words), 3 lines body, visual cue: (1) cover 'Same ₹22L offer, ₹4L gap in savings - here's why', (2) the assumption set (single, no dependents, 1BHK, eats out 3x/week, gym), (3) Bengaluru breakdown - rent ₹28K, transport ₹6K, food ₹12K, utilities ₹4K, lifestyle ₹15K, total monthly ₹65K, (4) Hyderabad breakdown - rent ₹22K, transport ₹5K, food ₹11K, utilities ₹4K, lifestyle ₹14K, total monthly ₹56K, (5) Mumbai breakdown - rent ₹42K, transport ₹3K, food ₹13K, utilities ₹4K, lifestyle ₹16K, total monthly ₹78K, (6) the savings table - in-hand vs spend vs save, with ₹4-5L annual delta, (7) the often-ignored cost of moving (security deposit ₹84K-₹1.7L, broker fee, packers ₹35-50K), (8) when Mumbai still wins (career compounding for finance, media), (9) my verdict by life stage and career, (10) CTA. Add 180-word LinkedIn post body and 4 hashtags. Tone: numbers-first, no city-bashing.

prompts.title: Health Insurance Audit Reel Script
prompts.prompt: Act as a personal finance creator on Instagram producing a 60-second Reel reviewing a follower's health insurance and giving 5 fixes. Here is the context: Audience is 28-38 salaried Indians with corporate cover plus a personal floater. Key 2026 facts: corporate cover often ends with the job, cashless network is shrinking at some hospitals due to PMJAY rate disputes, room rent capping is a stealth claim-killer, ₹10L sum insured is no longer enough in metro hospitals (a single ICU stay can cross ₹6L), super top-up plans cost 30-40% of base premium for an additional ₹40-90L cover, parental cover (60+) needs separate planning. Please write: (1) 0-5s hook 'Your ₹5L health policy is silently failing you. 5 fixes in 45 seconds.', (2) 5-15s problem 1 - sum insured under ₹15L in metros - with specific Apollo/Manipal ICU rate, (3) 15-25s problem 2 - room rent capping kills 30-40% of claim, (4) 25-35s problem 3 - reliance on corporate cover, with a 60-second portability tip, (5) 35-45s problem 4 - missing super top-up, with cost comparison ₹15K base + ₹6K top-up vs ₹35K standalone, (6) 45-55s problem 5 - parents on a separate floater, with senior-only plans like Niva Bupa Senior First, (7) 55-60s CTA. Add caption text, 4 trending audio suggestions, 3 on-screen overlays. Tone: urgent without fear-mongering.

prompts.title: Direct Stocks vs Mutual Fund Twitter Thread
prompts.prompt: Act as a personal finance creator with 130K followers on X writing a 10-tweet thread on whether retail investors should pick direct stocks or stick with mutual funds for the equity portion of their portfolio. Here is the context: SEBI's June 2025 study showed 73% of retail F&O traders lose money; a similar but milder loss-rate exists in cash equity for retail under 5 years of experience. Audience is 26-40 Indian professionals with ₹5-30L portfolios. Many feel mutual funds are 'boring' and want to add direct stocks after watching finfluencer P&L screenshots. Please write: (1) Tweet 1 - hook with a number 'Out of every 100 retail direct-stock pickers, 19 beat the NIFTY 50 over 5 years. The other 81 lost to a ₹0-effort index fund.', (2) Tweet 2 - the actual time investment of doing direct stocks well (annual reports, concalls, sector tracking), (3) Tweet 3 - the Indian mutual fund alpha reality - flexi-cap median manager beats NIFTY 500 TRI by 80-180 bps over 10 years, (4) Tweet 4 - the cost of mistakes - one wrong PSU bet vs a diversified MF, (5) Tweet 5 - the case for a 'core + satellite' approach, 80% MF + 20% direct, (6) Tweet 6 - the rules for the satellite (max 8 stocks, max 4% per stock, no F&O), (7) Tweet 7 - one specific behavioural trap - 'recency bias on momentum stocks', (8) Tweet 8 - tax inefficiency of frequent direct stock trading, (9) Tweet 9 - my opinion by experience level, (10) Tweet 10 - CTA. Each under 270 chars. End with 2 alt-hooks.

prompts.title: First Salary Money Decisions Reel Series
prompts.prompt: Act as a personal finance creator on Instagram with a Gen Z and young millennial audience. I need a 5-Reel series outline (each 45-60 seconds) called 'First Salary, First Mistakes' for fresh-out-of-college Indian engineers and analysts with ₹6-12 LPA offers starting July 2026. Here is the context: Most fresh hires blow their first 6 months on credit cards, lifestyle inflation, parents' gifts, and bad insurance from a relative. The series should set them up with 5 decisions in 5 days. Please deliver for each Reel: (1) the title, (2) the hook (under 12 words with a number), (3) the 3-segment script breakdown with timestamps, (4) the on-screen text overlays, (5) one B-roll cue, (6) the CTA to next Reel. The 5 Reels are: Reel 1 - Build ₹50K emergency fund in 2 months before any investment, Reel 2 - Get a ₹50L term plan and ₹10L health top-up before any SIP, Reel 3 - Start a ₹3K NIFTY 50 index SIP, not a smallcap one your friend swears by, Reel 4 - Choose new tax regime by default unless you have specific old-regime advantage, Reel 5 - Avoid the 3 lifestyle traps (premium credit card, EMI gadgets, friends-pressure travel). Add a series-launch carousel idea (5 slides) to drive Reel views. Tone: friend-not-lecturer, no preaching, no 'in today's economy'.

prompts.title: Quarterly Portfolio Review Newsletter Long-Form
prompts.prompt: Act as the lead writer for an Indian personal finance newsletter sent to 22,000 paid subscribers (₹699/month). I need a 1,600-word quarterly portfolio review email for Q4 FY26 (Jan-Mar 2026), structured around the model portfolio the newsletter publishes and tracks publicly. Here is the context: The model portfolio is 60% equity (35% Indian flexi-cap, 10% midcap index, 8% smallcap, 7% US S&P 500 feeder), 25% debt (15% short-duration MF, 10% G-Sec direct), 10% gold (5% SGB, 5% gold ETF), 5% cash. Q4 FY26 returns: portfolio +1.2%, NIFTY 500 -0.4%, NIFTY Smallcap 250 -7.1%, S&P 500 +2.1%, Gold +4.3%. Please structure: (1) subject line and preview text, (2) 100-word opening hook on what the quarter taught the portfolio, (3) attribution analysis - which sleeves added/subtracted, with bps contribution, (4) the smallcap drawdown discussion - whether to rebalance, with the rebalancing rule we wrote 18 months ago, (5) the US sleeve gain on USD/INR move - locking some via SWP, (6) Q1 FY27 outlook with 3 macro drivers (RBI, Fed, monsoon), (7) one specific buy and one specific trim with rationale and execution plan, (8) reader question of the quarter with 100-word answer, (9) sign-off and CTA. Use bold sub-heads. Include a SEBI-style disclaimer in footer. Add 2 subject line variants. Avoid hype words.

prompts.title: Podcast Episode Outline with Finance Author Guest
prompts.prompt: Act as the producer of a personal finance podcast in India with 75K weekly listeners, modelled on the Paisa Vaisa or Investing in India formats. I need a 50-minute episode outline with an Indian finance author guest like Monika Halan or Pattu (FreeFinCal) discussing 'why most Indian middle-class portfolios will fail by 2040'. Here is the context: Audience is 30-50 year-old salaried, with mid-sized portfolios ₹15-80L, who think they are doing well because their bank RM hasn't told them otherwise. The episode should be opinionated and challenge listeners. Please deliver: (1) episode title (under 12 words), (2) 80-word episode description for podcast platforms, (3) 50-minute segment breakdown - cold open hook 0:00-1:00, intros 1:00-3:00, segment 1 'what middle-class portfolios actually look like' 3:00-12:00 with 4 specific questions for the guest, segment 2 'the 5 silent killers (under-equity, over-FD, no health top-up, single asset class, late start)' 12:00-25:00 with 5 questions, segment 3 'what a fix looks like by age band' 25:00-38:00, segment 4 'rapid-fire opinions on direct stocks, F&O, gold, crypto, real estate' 38:00-45:00, listener Q&A 45:00-49:00, sign-off, (4) 12 specific questions to ask the guest, (5) 5 listener Q-prompts to seed in advance, (6) 3 promotional Reel clips identifying segments most clip-worthy, (7) sponsor read placement plan with exact timestamps, (8) episode SEO description 250 words. Tone: warm, rigorous.

prompts.title: Sponsorship Read Script for In-Video Integration
prompts.prompt: Act as a personal finance YouTube creator delivering a 75-second mid-roll sponsorship read for a tax-filing platform (think Cleartax, Quicko or TaxBuddy) inside a 12-minute video. Here is the context: My audience is 28-40 salaried, a third of whom have side income, ESOPs, or capital gains complicating their ITR. The brand wants the read to convert at 1.8%+ on the affiliate code. Sponsor wants me to mention: AI-assisted ITR-2/3 filing, capital gains import from Zerodha/Upstox, ₹399 starter / ₹999 pro tier, expert CA review add-on, my 20% off code 'CREATOR20'. The brand approves opinions; they do not approve hyperbole. Please write: (1) the spoken script of 75 seconds (180-200 words) starting from a natural transition, (2) 4 on-screen overlay text suggestions with timestamp, (3) 3 B-roll suggestions (laptop dashboard, capital gains report, CA on call), (4) the verbal CTA with the code mentioned twice in different ways, (5) the YouTube description block (3 lines + link), (6) 2 alternative versions (one for a video on ESOPs, one for a video on side hustles) with the same brand pitch tailored, (7) the moments to avoid placing the read (right before a verdict). Tone: honest, slightly self-deprecating about how I personally need this product. End with a 60-word disclosure note that satisfies the ASCI Influencer Guidelines.

prompts.title: Crypto vs Equity Honest Take Long-Form Outline
prompts.prompt: Act as a personal finance creator producing a 13-minute YouTube video outline taking an honest, math-led position on crypto vs equity for the average Indian investor in 2026. Here is the context: Audience is 26-40, exposed to crypto in 2021 mania, lost money in the 2022 winter, now watching BTC at hypothetical ₹95L per coin and wondering whether to allocate 5-10%. Key 2026 facts: India taxes crypto at flat 30% on gains with no loss set-off, 1% TDS on every transfer over ₹50K, no LTCG/STCG distinction, classified as VDA. Indian equity comparable: NIFTY 50 ~14% nominal CAGR over 20 years. Please structure: (1) 0:00-0:25 hook 'I would tell my own brother to keep crypto under 5% of his portfolio. Here's the math.', (2) 0:25-2:00 reset on what crypto is and isn't (not a hedge against inflation as marketed, mostly a beta-on-risk-appetite asset), (3) 2:00-4:00 the 2017-2025 data on BTC volatility, max drawdown 78%, and Sharpe ratio vs NIFTY 500, (4) 4:00-6:00 the Indian tax penalty - effective return after 30% tax + 1% TDS friction, (5) 6:00-8:00 the only 3 valid use cases (asymmetric upside small allocation, diversifier in a 25+ year horizon portfolio, USD-INR hedge proxy), (6) 8:00-10:00 the 5 things that would lose your money fast (leverage, alt coins, Telegram tips, Indian P2P off-ramps), (7) 10:00-12:00 my exact allocation guideline by net worth + age, (8) 12:00-13:00 verdict + CTA. Add 8 B-roll cues, 5 on-screen overlays, 3 alt thumbnails. Tone: skeptical but not dismissive.

prompts.title: 10-Slide LinkedIn Carousel on First-Time Home Buyer Math
prompts.prompt: Act as a LinkedIn personal finance creator with 175K followers writing a 10-slide carousel on the actual cost of a first home for a 32-year-old salaried Indian in Hyderabad in 2026. Here is the context: Audience is 28-40, considering a ₹85L flat in Kompally or Kondapur, with ₹17L savings, ₹28L annual in-hand, and a 7-year horizon. Most online calculators show only EMI, not the all-in cost. Please deliver each slide with headline (under 8 words), 3 lines body, visual cue: (1) cover with 'A ₹85L flat actually costs you ₹1.46 cr - here is the math', (2) the line items most buyers miss - stamp duty 5.5%, registration 0.5%, GST on under-construction 5%, brokerage 1%, interiors ₹10-18L, (3) the down payment reality - bank gives 80% LTV, so ₹17L cash needed plus charges totalling ₹26L, (4) the EMI math on ₹68L loan at 8.5% over 20 years = ₹59,012 monthly = ₹1.42 cr total payback, (5) the maintenance, property tax, society charges over 7 years ₹4.5-7L, (6) the opportunity cost of the ₹26L upfront if invested at 11%, (7) the 'rent-and-invest' scenario on the same flat (rent ₹26K/month vs EMI ₹59K, plus invest the differential), (8) when buying still wins (long stay, family, school zoning, settled job), (9) the 5 mistakes first-time buyers make, (10) my verdict by life stage + CTA. Add 180-word LinkedIn post body and 4 hashtags. Tone: numbers-first, anti-hype.

prompts.title: ITR Filing Season Twitter Thread for Salary + Capital Gains
prompts.prompt: Act as a personal finance creator with 110K followers on X writing during the ITR filing window for AY26-27 (April-July 2026). I need a 9-tweet thread on filing ITR-2 for salaried investors with capital gains, NRI clients, foreign assets, or ESOP perquisites. Here is the context: Audience is 28-45 salaried with mutual fund redemptions, direct stock sales, US stock holdings, ESOP exercises, FD interest, and dividend income. Most still try to use ITR-1 and risk a defective return notice u/s 139(9). Key 2026 facts: capital gains tax rates - LTCG equity 12.5% over ₹1.25L exemption, STCG equity 20%, debt MF held over 36 months at slab post 2023 amendment, US stocks LTCG over 24 months at 12.5% post-indexation removal; Schedule FA mandatory for foreign assets including RSUs vested abroad; Form 67 for foreign tax credit; AIS/TIS reconciliation matters. Please write: (1) Tweet 1 - hook 'If you redeemed even ₹1 of mutual fund this year, ITR-1 is wrong for you. Use ITR-2.', (2) Tweet 2 - who must file ITR-2 (5 triggers), (3) Tweet 3 - ESOP perquisite + capital gains dual reporting, (4) Tweet 4 - Schedule FA for US RSUs/ESOPs, with 80% of expat-returnees getting this wrong, (5) Tweet 5 - LTCG tax math on ₹3L gain example, (6) Tweet 6 - AIS reconciliation step before filing, (7) Tweet 7 - the dividend reporting trap, (8) Tweet 8 - 4 mistakes that trigger 139(9) defective notices, (9) Tweet 9 - my filing checklist + CTA. Each under 270 chars. End with 2 alt-hooks.

prompts.title: Annual Content Strategy Doc for Solo Finance Creator
prompts.prompt: Act as the content strategist for a solo personal finance creator in India approaching the FY26-27 content year (April 2026 - March 2027). I need a complete 12-month content strategy document covering YouTube long-form, Shorts, Instagram Reels and carousels, LinkedIn, X, newsletter, and one paid cohort, planned around the Indian financial calendar. Here is the context: Solo creator with 1 video editor, 1 part-time writer, 480K YT subs, 110K newsletter, 220K IG, 90K LinkedIn, 60K X. Annual revenue mix target: 40% sponsorship, 35% cohort, 15% AdSense, 10% affiliate. Indian calendar tentpoles: Apr (new FY, regime selection), Jun-Jul (ITR season), Aug-Sep (IPO season historically heavy), Oct-Nov (festival spend, Diwali Muhurat), Dec (year-end review, tax-loss harvest), Jan-Feb (Budget), Mar (last-mile tax-saving, ELSS deadline). Please deliver: (1) annual North Star metric and 4 leading metrics, (2) content pillar split (5 pillars with monthly weight), (3) tentpole calendar with one major series per quarter (e.g., Q1 'Tax Regime Decision', Q2 'ITR Bootcamp', Q3 'IPO Watchlist', Q4 'Budget + Year-End'), (4) team capacity plan (videos per week, Shorts per week, newsletters per week), (5) cohort schedule (2 cohorts in the year with launch windows), (6) sponsor pitch calendar by category (broker, MF house, insurance, fintech, tax-tech), (7) the 3 risks to plan for (AdSense slump, sponsor budget cuts, regulatory changes from SEBI/RBI), (8) one experiment per quarter (long YouTube, podcast crossover, premium newsletter tier), (9) a quarterly review cadence. Format as a 4-page doc. Tone: practical, founder-mode.
